title: andryala rothia subsp. ficalhoana
intro: Andryala rothia subsp. ficalhoana, also known as Ficalho's andryala, is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae, native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is known to offer various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Ficalho's andryala typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves and flowers, which can be consumed as teas or used topically in the form of ointments or salves.
